mappings.rationaleAI: Social class and stratification address the process of stratification (A.3), class, status, and power (A.3.b), theories of class (B.2), and social mobility (B.4).
Social differentiation addresses concepts of differentiation (A.1) and factors producing differences (A.2). Caste and status address B.1.
Aristocratic power addresses class, status, and power (A.3.b), class-to-elite relations (B.1), and upper class (B.3).
Class struggle addresses economic differentiation (A.3.a), theories of class (B.2), and classless society (B.5).
Class dynamics address economic differentiation (A.3.a), theories of class (B.2), and classless society (B.5).
Capital accumulation addresses economic differentiation (A.3.a), theories of class (B.2), and modern classes (B.3).
Racial differentiation addresses factors producing social differences (A.2).
Gender inequality addresses sex differentiation (A.2).
Gender intersecting with class and race addresses differentiation factors (A.2).
Hindu caste system addresses class-to-caste relation (B.1).
Chattel slavery addresses racial differentiation (A.2) and class-to-slavery relations (B.1).
Communist ideology addresses the classless society (B.5).
Collective ownership addresses theories of class (B.2) and equality of conditions (B.5).
Democracy addressing exclusion relates to effects of stratification (A.3.c) and equality (B.5).
Democratic society analysis addresses individual-society relation (A.3.c) and social mobility (B.4).
False consciousness provides perspectives on class importance (B.2).
Income distribution addresses economic differentiation (A.3.a) and modern classes (B.3).
National wealth comparisons address economic differentiation (A.3.a).
Prejudice and group dynamics address individual-society relation (A.3.c).
Mass mobilization addresses racial differentiation (A.2) and social mobility (B.4).
Identity formation addresses social factors (A.2) and individual-society relation (A.3.c).
People versus elite addresses class, status, and power (A.3.b) and class conceptions (B.2).
Caste dynamics address class-to-caste relation (B.1). Positive discrimination addresses social mobility (B.4).
Revolutions address social mobility through revolutionary change (B.4).
Industrialization's class impacts address economic differentiation (A.3.a) and modern classes (B.3).
